summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a compound inequality?

A compound inequality combines two inequalities using AND (intersection) or OR (union) to describe a solution set.

What is a 'bounded interval'?

A bounded interval has finite endpoints on both sides, such as 2 < x < 8. It is typically produced by AND compound inequalities.

What is an 'unbounded interval'?

An unbounded interval extends to infinity in one or both directions, like x > 3 or x < -2. OR compound inequalities often produce two unbounded intervals.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A student's grade will be a B if their average is at least 80 but less than 90. After three tests with scores 85, 78, and 88, the inequality 80 <= (85 + 78 + 88 + x)/4 < 90 gives the range for the 4th test. Solve for x.

Multiply by 4: 320 <= 251 + x < 360. Subtract 251: 69 <= x < 109.
